title: Double out_rc check separates transport error from server-side error in wolfHSM protocol
description: Every wolfHSM NVM and info call follows a pattern of checking two return codes: the FFI return code (transport-level: did the IPC call succeed?) and out_rc (server-level: did the server operation succeed?). For example, nvm_available calls WolfHsmError::check(rc, ...) then WolfHsmError::check(out_rc, ...(server)). The wolfHSM C API separates these because a successful IPC delivery can carry a server-side error response. Collapsing them into one check would silently ignore server-side errors when the IPC layer returned OK. A future contributor might try to simplify by only checking the FFI return value. nvm_list demonstrates why this matters further: WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND in out_rc is the normal end-of-list signal (not an error), and treating the FFI rc as the sole indicator would break the list termination logic.
